title: Holobiont Virus Extraction Protocol
author: Alex Veglia
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bmr5k586

steps.title: First, invertebrate samples taken at reef...
steps.instruction: First, invertebrate samples taken at reef or from aquarium must be rinsed with sterile seawater to mitigate potential contamination from ambient seawater. At end of step, organisms sit in sterile beaker with sterile seawater prior to tissue processing step

steps.title: Remove reef sourced sea water from...
steps.instruction: Remove reef sourced sea water from sampling container **wipe down outside of containers and area around drain/waste storage container (if on benchtop) ↓

steps.title: Refill the sampling container with sterile seawater (0
steps.instruction: Refill the sampling container with sterile seawater (0.2 micron (cellulose nitrate membrane) filter and autoclaved) **wipe down outside of containers and area around drain/waste storage container (if on benchtop)

steps.title: Now, vigorously shake and/or swirl the...
steps.instruction: Now, vigorously shake and/or swirl the sampling container, to remove/loosen any particulates or external mucus sheets from the invertabrate sample , for ~ 00:00:30

steps.title: After resting for around 00:02:00 ,...
steps.instruction: After resting for around 00:02:00 , swirl sample once more prior to decanting particulate- or mucus-containing seawater into a waste beaker

steps.title: Repeat Steps 2-4 two more times
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps 2-4 two more times

steps.title: After final round of rinsing, carefully...
steps.instruction: After final round of rinsing, carefully move the invertebrate sample to a sterile glass beaker containing sterile seawater OPTIONAL STEP -> Post-rinse, allow invertebrate to sit in sterile beaker with sterile seawater for 5 to 10 minutes to allow fitlering organisms like sponges or tunicates to "flush out" particles sourced from the reef prior to tissue processing

steps.title: Depending on the organism, tissue can...
steps.instruction: Depending on the organism, tissue can be seperated/processed in several ways. Generally, with fleshy organisms with or without a skeleton, a sterile razor blade can be used. A scalpel is sufficient as well, especially for softer organisms like sponges or seaslugs. **This step is usually done in triplicate, for example, three tissue aliquots of 0.2-0.3 mL processes per sampled invertebrate to allow for ample volume of virus particles for downstream manipulation (i.e. isolation) Example of 2 mL tube with ~.2-.3 mL of tissue at base, then the glass bead mixture, and the sterile medium at top before homogenization step

steps.title: On top of the tissue, 0
steps.instruction: On top of the tissue, 0.5 mL of a sterilized glass bead mixture (e.g. 0.1 g of 0.5 mm beads, 0.15 g of 0.1 mm beads, 0.4 g of 1.0 mm beads and three 1.3 mm beads) is added to the 2 mL centrifuge tube ** the glass bead mixture should contain three-four different sizes to target a majority of cellular organisms within invertebrate tissue

steps.title: Fill 2 mL centrifuge tube containing...
steps.instruction: Fill 2 mL centrifuge tube containing glass beads and invertebrate tissue with sterile seawater to the 2 mL mark

steps.title: Seal the tops of 2 mL...
steps.instruction: Seal the tops of 2 mL centrifuge tubes with parafilm prior to homogenization step to mitigate the chance of them opening and spilling

steps.title: For the homogenization step a vortex...
steps.instruction: For the homogenization step a vortex or a FastPrep machine can be used. Between cycles of disruption, be sure to put samples on ice. Example of what a sample may look like post homogenization

steps.title: Secure the 2 mL centrifuge tubes...
steps.instruction: Secure the 2 mL centrifuge tubes containing, glass beads + tissue + sterile seawater, to a vortex machine or a FastPrep™ instrument (Thermo Electron Corporation; FP

steps.title: Vortex -> On highest setting (10),...
steps.instruction: Vortex -> On highest setting (10), run vortex for 00:05:00 OR FastPrep -> Run for 00:00:20

steps.title: Let samples sit on ice for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Let samples sit on ice for 00:01:00

steps.title: Repeat Steps 12-13 three times (example result is pictured above)
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps 12-13 three times (example result is pictured above)

steps.title: Centrifuge samples for 00:16:00 at 3
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples for 00:16:00 at 3.200 x g, 4 °C,

steps.title: Transfer as much supernatant as possilbe...
steps.instruction: Transfer as much supernatant as possilbe to a new, sterile 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and if low volume, dilute to ~1.2 mL mark with sterile seawater

steps.title: Centrifuge sample again for 00:17:00 at 16
steps.instruction: Centrifuge sample again for 00:17:00 at 16.000 x g, 4 °C,

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to new, sterile 1
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to new, sterile 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and dilute to 1.0 mL mark, with sterile seawater, before moving to the next step

steps.title: Add 250-300 ul of PEG (polyethylene...
steps.instruction: Add 250-300 ul of PEG (polyethylene glycol 5x; BioVision - Catalog #K

steps.title: to the newly tranferred supernatant in the sterile 1
steps.instruction: to the newly tranferred supernatant in the sterile 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Let the 1
steps.instruction: Let the 1.5 mL centrifuge tube with the supernatant + PEG sit overnight (no less than 12 hours) at 4 degrees C

steps.title: After the overnight incubation, centrifuge the...
steps.instruction: After the overnight incubation, centrifuge the sample for 00:30:00 at 3.200 x g, 4 °C,

steps.title: Very carefully, remove the supergnatant without...
steps.instruction: Very carefully, remove the supergnatant without disturbing the pellet using a pipette, or by carefully decanting to a waste container.

steps.title: Re-suspend pellet in atleast ~1 mL...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend pellet in atleast ~1 mL of sterile seawater or to keep in high concetration, in ~200 ul of virus resuspension liquid (BioVision - Catalog #K904). At this point, depending on size of pellet, this solution can be diluted further to increase the volume usable for isolation efforts.
